data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-89ab3fda">
    <title>work: introduce StopCondition classifier for drain exits</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The C9 bead ddx-9228a484 is blocked because it bundles every stop/retry concern into one oversized pass. The first executable slice is to create the work-layer StopCondition vocabulary and wire the drain loop to use it for terminal drain exits before moving budget, no-progress, review repair, or trace behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:504-508 documents exit reasons as ad hoc strings inside ExecuteBeadWorker.Run instead of using a typed stop classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:550-560 checks context cancellation inline during every drain iteration and sets exit_reason directly.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1355-1357 handles --once completion inline with another direct return.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1789-1813 currently lets the executor closure synthesize a cost-cap failure report, so budget stopping is not represented as a first-class work-layer stop condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with a StopCondition enum and helper that classifies drain exit decisions.
- Required enum values: Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Wire ExecuteBeadWorker.Run to call the helper for the already-existing terminal decisions it owns today: context cancellation/signal, --once completion, explicit poll-zero/drained behavior, and budget stop handoff if a budget stop is provided by the caller.
- Keep behavior-compatible loop.end exit_reason strings, but derive them from the typed stop condition instead of scattering literals.
- Leave cost-cap relocation, no-progress eligibility, review terminal taxonomy, repair cycles, and execution trace schema to sibling split beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change retry budgets or no-progress eligibility in this bead.
- Do not implement review repair cycles.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model selection, or MinPower escalation.
- Do not rename ExecuteBeadWorker or move the whole drain loop.

DEPS
No deps. The closed dependencies on ddx-9228a484 already landed; this is the first split slice.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition with exact values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, and Blocked.
2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run uses the StopCondition helper for context cancellation/signal, --once completion, drained/poll-zero exit, and caller-provided budget stop decisions instead of setting those terminal decisions entirely inline.
3. TestStopCondition_ClassifiesDrainExitReasons covers Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, and Blocked.
4. TestExecuteBeadWorkerStopConditionWiredIntoRun proves production Run emits the same loop.end exit_reason values through the StopCondition path.
5. Existing TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown and TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop still pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestStopCondition|TestExecuteBeadWorkerStopCondition|TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown|TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, area:work, kind:refactor, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, split-from:ddx-9228a484</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="04c047a8adb7263c55462bd48295b5e2cd30b934" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T174102-42ee6315"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
The bead was already completed by a parallel execution. Looking at the git log on `main`:

- `8a1e45f68 test(work): cover pre-claim stop-condition fallout [ddx-89ab3fda]` — fixed the 4 pre-existing test failures (updated MaxPower assertion, added intake stubs to try and resource exhaustion tests)
- `d5bf41daa chore: close ddx-89ab3fda` — bead closed

The parallel execution committed exactly the fixes needed: the `work/stop.go` + test files were already in the base checkpoint, and the four failing tests were fixed by updating the stale MaxPower assertion and adding intake stub handlers to the try/resource-exhaustion tests.

The worktree for this session was cleaned up after the other execution merged. All ACs are satisfied on `main`.
